item.title: GM idles Detroit EV plant, temporarily laying off 1,300
item.description: General Motors вҖӢis idling вҖҢa Detroit electric vehicle вҖӢplant until вҖӢApril 13, extending вҒ downtime that вҖӢbegan on вҖӢMarch 16, the company said вҖӢon Monday.

item.title: TRADING DAY Growth fears snowball
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit seven-month lows and bond yields fell on Monday as the Iran war entered its fifth week, with investors increasingly spooked by growth fears over inflation concerns even though oil prices rose further above $100 a barrel.

item.title: Fed's Williams says monetary policy 'well positioned' for 'unusual' circumstances
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said on Monday that the current setting of monetary policy is in вҖӢa good place to deal with a range of challenges that very likely herald higher near-term inflation.

item.title: Verizon wins injunction blocking T-Mobile ad campaign promising big savings
item.description: A federal judge вҖҢon Monday granted Verizon Wireless a preliminary injunction blocking rival T-Mobile from running ads promising consumers more than $1,000 of annual savings if they switch cellphone carriers.

item.title: US business equipment borrowings rise more than 14% in February, ELFA says
item.description: U.S. companies borrowed 14.2% more to finance equipment purchases in February than a вҖӢyear earlier, fueled by a surge in вҖҢactivity among independent providers, the Equipment Leasing and Finance Association said on Monday.

item.title: Big Tech sports broadcast rights could imperil US local TV news, stations say
item.description: Major broadcast station owners on Monday said вҖӢU.S. regulators should вҖҢaddress the trend of Big Tech companies acquiring the вҖӢrights to broadcast вҖӢfootball, baseball and other вҒ sporting events, saying it вҖӢcould weaken local TV вҖӢnews.

item.title: JPMorgan must face Wells Fargo lawsuit over troubled $481 million real estate loan
item.description: A federal judge on Monday rejected JPMorgan вҖҢChase's bid to dismiss Wells Fargo's breach of contract lawsuit to recoup losses for investors in a defaulted $481 million commercial real estate loan.

item.title: Delaware judge accused of bias reassigns Musk cases
item.description: The chief judge on Delaware's corporate court said she will reassign three cases involving Elon Musk вҖӢto avoid unnecessary media attention after the billionaire entrepreneur complained that her activity on social media had shown bias вҖҢagainst him.

item.title: Exclusive: E*Trade in talks to lead SpaceX IPO share sale to small investors in US, sources say
item.description: Morgan Stanley's E*Trade is in talks вҖӢwith SpaceX to take the lead in selling the rocket maker's shares to everyday U.S. investors in its highly anticipated IPO later вҖҢthis year, giving it an edge over rival brokerages Robinhood Markets and SoFi, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fed watching private credit sector for signs of trouble, Powell says
item.description: Federal Reserve вҖӢChair Jerome Powell said on Monday the U.S. вҖҢcentral bank is watching developments in the private credit sector for signs of trouble, but does not currently see issues there bringing вҖӢdown the financial system as a whole.

item.title: Italy data protection agency fines Intesa Sanpaolo $36 mln over data breach
item.description: Italy's data вҖӢprotection authority said on Monday it вҖҢhad fined the country's biggest bank Intesa Sanpaolo 31.8 million euro ($36.41 million) over a data breach вҖӢcase that involved some 3,500 customers вҖӢover two years.

item.title: Crypto fuels drone purchases in Russia and Iran, report says
item.description: Groups linked to Russia and Iran вҖӢare increasingly using cryptocurrency to finance the purchase of low-cost military drones and components, вҖҢaccording to a new report from blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis.

item.title: Components maker Elmet discloses revenue rise in US IPO filing
item.description: Precision-engineered components manufacturer Elmet Group on Monday revealed higher revenue for 2025 in its filing for an initial public вҖӢoffering in the United States, as it looks to вҖҢtap investor interest in defense-linked businesses.

item.title: US issues draft rules on private assets in 401k plans
item.description: The U.S. Department of Labor on Monday issued long-awaited proposed new rules intended to clarify how trustees can add alternative assets ranging from private вҖӢequity to cryptocurrencies to 401(k) retirement plans.

item.title: India approves over $750 million in projects for electronic component manufacturing
item.description: India on Monday approved 29 proposals from various companies under its вҖӢelectronics component manufacturing program, with a total вҖҢinvestment of 71.04 billion rupees ($751.21 million), the Electronics and Information Technology Ministry said.

item.title: Voyager, Icarus Robotics to test free-flying robot on space station
item.description: Space-tech firm Voyager Technologies has secured a mission management contract with robotics startup Icarus вҖӢRobotics to demonstrate a free-flying robotic system on вҖҢthe International Space Station (ISS), the companies said on Monday.

item.title: Match Group settles US FTC claims it illegally shared OkCupid user data
item.description: Match Group settled a U.S. Federal Trade вҖӢCommission lawsuit accusing it вҖҢof giving an outside company access to personal вҖӢdata of millions вҖӢof OkCupid users including photos, вҒ demographic information and вҖӢlocation information, a court filing вҖӢshows.

item.title: China Southern returns to profit in 2025 after five years of losses
item.description: China Southern Airlines , on Monday reported a net profit вҖӢof 857 million yuan ($124.01 million) for вҖҢ2025, returning to the black for the first time since 2019, as it вҖӢbenefited from operational improvements and tighter вҖӢcost controls.

item.title: Bessent says nearly half of US tax filers claim new deductions in Republican tax law
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Monday that individual tax refunds during the 2026 tax filing season are вҖӢup more than 10% over last year, with nearly вҖҢhalf of filers claiming new deductions offered in the Republican tax cut law passed last year.

item.title: US Department of Labor issues 401k guidelines on private assets
item.description: The U.S. Department of Labor on Monday вҖӢissued long-awaited proposed new rules intended вҖҢto clarify how trustees can add alternative assets ranging from private equity to вҖӢcryptocurrencies to 401(k) retirement plans.

item.title: China's BYD confident of reaching 1.5 million unit overseas sales in 2026
item.description: BYD is "highly confident" of reaching its 2026 overseas sales target of 1.5 million vehicles or even higher, the вҖӢcompany told analysts during its post-earnings call on Monday.

item.title: GM to increase heavy-duty truck production, WSJ reports
item.description: General вҖӢMotors вҖҢwill вҖӢincrease вҖӢthe production вҒ of вҖӢits вҖӢheavy-duty trucks, вҖӢthe вҖӢWall Street вҖҢJournal вҒ reported вҖӢon вҖӢMonday.

item.title: Starbucks investors reelect full board, rejecting labor-backed challenge
item.description: In a triumph for StarbucksвҖҷ board of directors over вҖҢa coalition of labor-focused shareholder groups, investors this week overwhelmingly reelected two directors accused by the groups of turning a blind eye to risky labor relations.

item.title: Alaska Air signals bigger first-quarter loss as fuel costs surge
item.description: Alaska Air Group on Monday estimated a bigger first-quarter loss as higher fuel prices due to the Iran вҖӢwar added to pressure from weaker demand in parts вҖҢof its network.

item.title: Xerox names insider Louie Pastor as new CEO after Bandrowczak exits
item.description: Xerox Holdings on Monday named insider Louie Pastor вҖӢas its new chief вҖҢexecutive effective immediately, after Steve Bandrowczak stepped down.

item.title: South Korea's AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million in latest funding round
item.description: South Korean artificial intelligence chip startup Rebellions said on Monday it had raised $400 million in its latest funding round, valuing the вҖӢcompany at about $2.34 billion as it accelerates expansion into the вҖҢU.S.

item.title: Microsoft unveils AI upgrades, rolls out Copilot Cowork to early-access customers
item.description: Microsoft on Monday unveiled new features in its Copilot research assistant that would allow users to utilize multiple AI models вҖӢsimultaneously within the same workflow, the latest move by the tech вҖҢgiant to improve its AI offering and boost adoption.

item.title: Priced low, growing fast: women's sports draw smart money
item.description: Wealthy investors are increasingly turning to women's sports as a rare opportunity to tap into вҖӢa fast-growing market at lower valuations, favoring strong returns over the high entry costs and limited upside of marquee men's franchises in the NFL or вҖҢthe UK's Premier League.

item.title: What G7 countries are doing to cap energy prices
item.description: Energy prices around the world have soared after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to U.S.-Israeli attacks, leaving many Group of Seven and EU governments seeking ways to lessen the impact on their economies.

item.title: India proposes making government advisories legally binding on tech giants
item.description: India on Tuesday proposed changes to its IT law to make advisories вҖӢand clarifications legally binding on internet platforms вҖҢsuch as Meta , Google and X, the latest in a string of stricter compliance requirements for tech вҖӢgiants.
